questions.question: What is the purpose of inserting records into a table in a database?
questions.explanation: Inserting records into a table allows you to add new data to the database.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a record in a table?
questions.explanation: A record in a table is a collection of values in the form of rows and columns.

questions.question: What is the syntax for inserting a record into a table in SQL?
options: INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2) VALUES (value1, value2);
questions.explanation: The correct syntax for inserting a record into a table in SQL is INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2) VALUES (value1, value2);

questions.question: Which of the following data types is suitable for storing names in a database?
questions.explanation: String data type is suitable for storing names, dates, and other non-numeric data.

questions.question: What happens when you insert a duplicate record into a table?
options: D) The record is inserted again
questions.explanation: When you insert a duplicate record into a table, the record is ignored. You can use the UNIQUE constraint to prevent duplicate records.
